title: AR-SAT Room

fields.vision: AR-SAT Room is a design-and-schema handoff for an adversarially robust structured-analytic-technique room.
fields.mechanism: It routes an intelligence problem through a Room View that binds triad method, products, and proof; Idea Refinery, Patch Forge, baseline-comparison Evaluation Harness, JSON-Schema entities, command-palette behavior, station drawer, audit timeline, evidence handling, adversarial stress, and reviewer workflow make SAT use inspectable rather than decorative. The handoff names React/Next/Vite as the intended componentization path, while backend, auth, persistence, and vector search remain outside this pass.
fields.built_components: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.source_utilization: Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where available. This strengthens page vision, mechanism, source-use, plan, and gap language, but it does not certify passage-level citation support. source-library controlled content is source evidence for internal scholarship and planning; do not redistribute source files or long excerpts in the public package.
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: You are staffing up a product/design team to build the flagship public AR-SAT/CDIS v2 demonstrator, or you need a recruiter-facing job-fair deck that walks through the analytic workflow end-to-end. Use it as the design contract for a Figma build and backlog creation. Entry point: prototype/index.html for the narrative; docs/RoomView_UIUX_Spec.md for the spec; schemas/idea_object.schema.json for the canonical entity. Integration target: Not wired to MuranoOS or a specific repo. Suggested next engineering pass: convert prototype into React/Vite app, wire to schemas + sample data, implement command palette + station drawers + audit timeline as stateful components. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None production. Only static HTML prototypes (prototype/index.html, prototype/room-view_prototype_singlefile.html) illustrating layout and interactions. No backend, no framework code, no tests.
fields.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: This pack is the v0.1 design + schema handoff for AR-SAT (Adversarially-Robust SATs) / CDIS v2, an intelligence-community workflow platform whose flagship screen is the "Room View" — a single cartographic page that presents, at a glance, the triad method — products — proof. The room is an auditable interdisciplinary Idea Refinery, a Patch Forge for deployable SAT work products, and a baseline-comparison Evaluation Harness with governance controls. The intended audience is a front-end / product engineering team that will convert the static HTML mock into a componentized app (React/Next/Vite), wire it to the three JSON-Schema-defined entities, and implement command-palette / station-drawer / audit-timeline behaviors as stateful components. Backend, auth, persistence, and vector search are explicitly out of scope for this pass.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: AR-SAT Room is a design-and-schema handoff for an adversarially robust structured-analytic-technique room.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It routes an intelligence problem through a Room View that binds triad method, products, and proof; Idea Refinery, Patch Forge, baseline-comparison Evaluation Harness, JSON-Schema entities, command-palette behavior, station drawer, audit timeline, evidence handling, adversarial stress, and reviewer workflow make SAT use inspectable rather than decorative. The handoff names React/Next/Vite as the intended componentization path, while backend, auth, persistence, and vector search remain outside this pass.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because structured analytic techniques become more useful when their inputs, adversarial failure modes, interface logic, data model, and review obligations are built into the workspace.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

instruments.output: a room, workbench, or interface function tied to `Room View`

instruments.downstream_use: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: You are staffing up a product/design team to build the flagship public AR-SAT/CDIS v2 demonstrator, or you need a recruiter-facing.
instruments.proof: This pack is the v0.1 design + schema handoff for AR-SAT (Adversarially-Robust SATs) / CDIS v2, an intelligence-community workflow platform whose flagship screen is the "Room View" — a single cartographic page that presents, at a glance, the triad method —.
instruments.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
instruments.gap: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved.
instruments.verification_check: Confirm `Room View` appears in the cited source context and that the public page explains what output, decision, or project function it supports.
